title: EinsteinEquation
summary: Packages the Einstein equation and parity/expansion certificates used by later PE constructions.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Packages the Einstein equation and parity/expansion certificates used by later PE constructions.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formal boundary
mathematicalContent.body: This page describes exactly the declarations present in Ambient/PE/EinsteinEquation.lean; claims not represented by a declaration are not counted as verified.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: can. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem can construct these certificates from ambient Ricci-flatness. -/

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Data/interface: PEFormal Expansion. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Definition: Has Even Expansion To Order. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Formal proposition: has Even Expansion To Boundary Order. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Data/interface: Is Poincare Einstein. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: structure IsPoincareEinstein {X : PESpace.{u, v}} (NF : PoincareNormalFormData.{u, v, w} X)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: PE expansion even to order. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem PE_expansion_even_to_order {X : PESpace} {NF : PoincareNormalFormData X} (hPE : IsPoincareEinstein NF) : hPE.formalExpansion.HasEvenExpansionToOrder X.boundaryDim

references.note: Source for Poincaré normal form and its relation to straight ambient metrics.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
